column4: Validate that API Gateway accepts a valid Cognito-issued JWT and returns an authenticated response
column5: Cognito User Pool deployed; test user exists in PreSales group; API Gateway Cognito authoriser configured
column6:
1. Obtain JWT access token from Cognito for test PreSales user
2. Send GET /api/v1/health with Authorization: Bearer <token>
3. Verify HTTP 200 response
4. Verify token claims match expected user attributes
column7: HTTP 200 with authenticated response; token sub and cognito:groups claims correct
column13: DDD §Security – Identity & Access Management; IG §Security – Deployment Steps
column14: Test all three Cognito groups: AmAdmin/PreSales/Delivery

column4: Validate that API Gateway rejects missing or invalid Cognito JWT tokens
column5: API Gateway Cognito authoriser configured; WAF WebACL active
column6:
1. Send POST /api/v1/solutions with no Authorization header
2. Send request with an expired token
3. Send request with a tampered token signature
4. Verify response codes for each scenario
column7: HTTP 401 for all three unauthorised attempts; no Lambda function invoked
column13: DDD §Security – Identity & Access Management; DDD §Integration Design – Authentication & SSO Flows
column14: Verify CloudTrail logs each rejected attempt

column4: Validate that the isb-api-submit Lambda enforces request payload schema
column5: API Gateway and isb-api-submit Lambda deployed; valid Cognito JWT available
column6:
1. Send POST /api/v1/solutions with missing required fields
2. Send request with invalid data types in brief payload
3. Send request with brief payload exceeding 512 KB
4. Send request with all required fields valid
column7: HTTP 400 with descriptive error body for cases 1-3; HTTP 202 with solution_id for case 4
column13: DDD §Integration Design – API Design; IG §Application Configuration – Environment Variable Reference
column14: Max brief size is 512 KB per application config

column4: Validate that a valid brief submission is written to SolutionState and enqueued on the SQS job queue
column5: isb-api-submit Lambda deployed; DynamoDB AmatraISB-SolutionState table active; SQS amatra-isb-job-queue provisioned
column6:
1. Submit valid brief via POST /api/v1/solutions with valid JWT
2. Verify HTTP 202 and solution_id in response
3. Query DynamoDB SolutionState for the returned solution_id
4. Verify SQS queue depth incremented by 1 after submission
column7: HTTP 202 returned within 500 ms p99; DynamoDB record created with status PENDING; SQS message visible in queue
column13: DDD §Data Architecture – Data Flow Design Step 1; DDD §Component Design
column14: Monitor provisioned concurrency on isb-api-submit

column4: Validate that the isb-orchestrator-start Lambda consumes the SQS message and starts a Step Functions execution
column5: SQS job queue populated with a test job message; Step Functions state machine active; isb-orchestrator-start Lambda deployed
column6:
1. Place a synthetic job message on amatra-isb-job-queue-staging
2. Wait for Lambda SQS trigger (≤5 s)
3. Query Step Functions for active executions
4. Verify DynamoDB SolutionState record updated to IN_PROGRESS
column7: Step Functions execution starts within 5 s of SQS message delivery; DynamoDB status updated to IN_PROGRESS
column13: DDD §Data Architecture – Data Flow Design Step 2; DDD §Infrastructure – Compute Sizing
column14: Verify execution input contains solution_id and artifact_type_list

column4: Validate that isb-api-submit blocks submissions when the per-user monthly generation limit is reached
column5: DynamoDB AmatraISB-UsageTracking table populated with a user at their monthly limit; valid JWT for that user
column6:
1. Set user generation_count equal to per_user_monthly_default (10)
2. Submit a new brief via POST /api/v1/solutions
3. Verify HTTP 429 response
4. Verify DynamoDB UsageTracking record was NOT modified
column7: HTTP 429 returned; DynamoDB record unchanged; no SQS message enqueued
column13: DDD §Data Architecture – Logical Model UsageCounter; DDD §Integration Design – API Design GET /api/v1/usage/me
column14: Test admin override path separately in FT-013

column4: Validate that isb-bedrock-sonnet Lambda successfully invokes Amazon Bedrock Claude 3 Sonnet and returns generated content
column5: Bedrock Claude 3 Sonnet model enabled in us-west-2; isb-bedrock-sonnet Lambda deployed with correct IAM execution role; test prompt available in S3 templates bucket
column6:
1. Invoke isb-bedrock-sonnet directly with a test prompt payload
2. Verify Lambda returns within 900 s timeout
3. Verify response body contains generated text content
4. Verify Bedrock token usage is captured in the response metadata
column7: Generated artifact content returned; no ThrottlingException; token count recorded in Lambda response
column13: DDD §Component Design – AI Generation Engine; DDD §Technology Stack – AI/ML Generation
column14: Use a representative SOW-type prompt; verify Sonnet model ID matches config

column4: Validate that isb-bedrock-haiku Lambda successfully invokes Amazon Bedrock Claude 3 Haiku for lightweight artifact types
column5: Bedrock Claude 3 Haiku model enabled in us-west-2; isb-bedrock-haiku Lambda deployed; test discovery-questionnaire prompt in S3
column6:
1. Invoke isb-bedrock-haiku with a discovery-questionnaire prompt payload
2. Verify Lambda returns within 600 s timeout
3. Verify generated content matches expected questionnaire structure
4. Compare cost (token count) to Sonnet equivalent
column7: Generated questionnaire content returned; token count lower than Sonnet baseline for same prompt; no errors
column13: DDD §Component Design – AI Generation Engine; DDD §Technology Stack
column14: Haiku used for discovery-questionnaire and infrastructure-costs types

column4: Validate that isb-artifact-processor writes the generated artifact to S3 under the correct key taxonomy and updates DynamoDB SolutionState
column5: isb-artifact-processor Lambda deployed; S3 artifacts bucket provisioned with SSE-KMS; DynamoDB SolutionState table active
column6:
1. Invoke isb-artifact-processor with synthetic Bedrock output and solution_id
2. Verify S3 object created at key {solution_id}/raw/pre-sales/{artifact_type}/{filename}
3. Verify DynamoDB SolutionState record updated with artifact S3 key
4. Verify S3 object metadata includes SSE-KMS encryption
column7: S3 object present at correct key; DynamoDB s3_key attribute populated; S3 object server-side encrypted with CMK alias/amatra-isb-s3-artifacts-prod
column13: DDD §Data Architecture – Data Flow Design Steps 5-6; DDD §Data Architecture – Data Governance
column14: Test with all 7 artifact types to confirm correct key paths

column4: Validate that GET /api/v1/solutions/{solution_id}/artifacts/{type} returns a valid 24-hour presigned S3 URL
column5: DynamoDB SolutionState record exists with status COMPLETE and artifact s3_key populated; isb-api-retrieve Lambda deployed with valid IAM role
column6:
1. Authenticate and call GET /api/v1/solutions/{solution_id}/artifacts/solution-briefing
2. Verify HTTP 200 with presigned_url in response body
3. Download the artifact via the presigned URL
4. Verify the presigned URL expires after 24 hours
column7: HTTP 200 returned; presigned URL valid and downloadable; URL expires at expected TTL of 86400 s per application config
column13: DDD §Integration Design – API Design GET artifacts/{type}; IG §Application Configuration – presigned_url_ttl_seconds
column14: Verify unauthenticated access to URL succeeds; authenticated access to expired URL returns 403

column3: End-to-End Pre-Sales Pipeline – All 4 Phase 1 Artifact Types
column4: Validate the complete brief-to-artifact generation pipeline for all four Phase 1 artifact types in one submission
column5: Full Phase 1 platform deployed in Staging; Cognito user with PreSales group available; representative synthetic client brief prepared
column6:
1. Submit client brief via POST /api/v1/solutions specifying all 4 Phase 1 artifact types
2. Poll GET /api/v1/solutions/{solution_id}/status until COMPLETE or timeout at 60 min
3. Retrieve presigned URLs for each artifact type
4. Download and inspect each artifact for required sections
5. Verify QA scores in DynamoDB SolutionState
column7: All 4 artifacts (discovery-questionnaire; solution-briefing; statement-of-work; infrastructure-costs) generated within 60 min; all required sections present; no placeholder text remaining
column13: DDD §Implementation Approach – Phase 1c Exit Criteria; SOW §Testing & Validation – Functional Validation
column14: Primary Phase 1 UAT gateway test

column3: End-to-End Delivery Pipeline – All 3 Phase 2 Artifact Types
column4: Validate the complete brief-to-artifact generation pipeline for all three Phase 2 delivery artifact types
column5: Full Phase 2 platform deployed in Staging; Cognito user with Delivery group; representative synthetic client brief prepared
column6:
1. Submit client brief specifying detailed-design; implementation-guide; terraform artifact types
2. Poll status until COMPLETE or 60-min timeout
3. Retrieve and download all 3 artifacts
4. Verify Terraform HCL output is syntactically valid using terraform validate
5. Verify QA validation layer score ≥ 90% in DynamoDB
column7: All 3 delivery artifacts generated within 60 min; terraform validate returns success; QA score ≥ 90%; all required document sections present
column13: DDD §Implementation Approach – Phase 2 Exit Criteria; DDD §Data Architecture – Data Governance
column14: Run terraform validate in an isolated workspace

column4: Validate that an AmAdmin user can override a per-user monthly generation limit and the affected user can then submit a new job
column5: DynamoDB UsageTracking record for target user at monthly limit; Cognito user in AmAdmin group available
column6:
1. Authenticate as AmAdmin and call PUT /api/v1/usage/users/{user_id}/limit with new limit value
2. Verify HTTP 200 and updated limit value in response
3. Authenticate as the target user and submit a new brief
4. Verify job enqueued and generation proceeds normally
5. Verify admin action recorded in CloudTrail
column7: HTTP 200 on override; DynamoDB UsageTracking record reflects new limit; subsequent job submission succeeds; CloudTrail event for PUT /api/v1/usage logged
column13: DDD §Integration Design – API Design PUT /api/v1/usage/users/{user_id}/limit; DDD §Security – Identity & Access Management – Role Definitions
column14: Verify AmAdmin CloudTrail event includes caller identity

column4: Validate platform handles 24 concurrent brief submissions without errors or data conflicts
column5: Full platform deployed in Staging with Locust or pytest fixtures; 24 distinct synthetic brief payloads prepared; DynamoDB on-demand capacity active
column6:
1. Submit 24 brief generation jobs simultaneously from 24 different test user sessions
2. Poll status for all 24 solution_ids
3. Monitor SQS queue depth; DLQ depth; Step Functions active executions
4. Wait for all 24 jobs to reach COMPLETE or FAILED within 60 min
column7: All 24 jobs reach COMPLETE within 60 min; DLQ depth remains 0; no DynamoDB throttle events; no Lambda throttle events
column13: DDD §Business Context – Workload Criticality (24 concurrent jobs); SOW §Testing & Validation – Performance & Load Testing
column14: Corresponds to the quarterly throughput target of 24 engagements

column4: Validate that reverting Lambda PROD aliases to a prior verified version restores platform functionality within 30 minutes
column5: Two verified Lambda deployment versions exist in Production (or Staging); GitHub Actions rollback.yml workflow available
column6:
1. Record current PROD alias version for all API Handler functions
2. Deploy a synthetic defective Lambda version and update PROD aliases
3. Execute rollback.yml workflow targeting the prior version
4. Verify PROD aliases revert to prior version
5. Confirm POST /api/v1/solutions returns HTTP 202 after rollback
column7: All PROD aliases reverted to prior version within 30 min; health endpoint returns HTTP 200; end-to-end brief submission succeeds post-rollback
column13: DDD §Implementation Approach – Rollback Strategy; IG §Migration & Cutover – Rollback Procedure
column14: Rollback must complete in ≤30 min per SOW RTO commitment

column4: Validate that the Template Ingestion Lambda parses a Word/Excel/PowerPoint template and produces a valid Bedrock prompt template in S3
column5: isb-template-ingest Lambda deployed; sample DOCX/XLSX/PPTX templates uploaded to S3 under templates/legacy/
column6:
1. Upload a representative DOCX template to s3://amatra-isb-templates-staging/legacy/solution-briefing/
2. Trigger isb-template-ingest Lambda manually with S3 event payload
3. Verify extracted prompt template written to S3 under templates/prompts/solution-briefing/
4. Submit a brief using the new prompt and verify artifact is generated
column7: Prompt template created in S3 without error; downstream generation job completes with ≥90% QA score
column13: DDD §Data Architecture – Data Migration Strategy – Legacy Template Pipeline; IG §Prerequisites – Technical Prerequisites
column14: Test DOCX; XLSX; and PPTX formats separately

column3: API Gateway Response Time Under Load
column4: Validate that the job submission endpoint meets the ≤500 ms p99 response time target under 24 concurrent users
column5: Staging platform deployed; Locust load test configured; provisioned concurrency active on isb-api-submit (5 units)
column6:
1. Configure Locust with 24 concurrent users and 2 user/s spawn rate
2. Execute 30-min sustained load test against POST /api/v1/solutions
3. Collect API Gateway latency metrics from CloudWatch
4. Calculate p99 response time from latency distribution
column7: p99 response time ≤500 ms for POST /api/v1/solutions throughout the 30-min run; zero Lambda cold-start errors on provisioned concurrency functions
column13: DDD §Business Context – Workload Criticality – API Response Time ≤500 ms p99; SOW §Testing & Validation – Performance & Load Testing
column14: Baseline before Phase 1 go-live; repeat after Phase 2 go-live

column3: Async Job Completion Under Peak Load
column4: Validate that all 24 concurrent generation jobs complete within the 60-minute SLA under sustained peak load
column5: Full platform deployed in Staging; Locust load test configured for 24 concurrent job submissions; Step Functions state machine active
column6:
1. Submit 24 concurrent generation jobs via Locust at spawn rate of 2 users/s
2. Poll Step Functions execution statuses every 60 s
3. Record completion time for each execution
4. Monitor DLQ depth and Lambda error rates throughout
column7: All 24 jobs reach COMPLETE status within 60 min; DLQ depth remains 0; Lambda error rate < 1%; no Step Functions execution failures
column13: DDD §Business Context – Workload Criticality – Async Job Completion ≤60 min p95; SOW §Testing & Validation – Performance & Load Testing
column14: 2× expected peak per SOW performance test requirement

column4: Validate that Lambda cold-start latency is ≤3 seconds for all four API Handler functions under production-like conditions
column5: API Handler Lambda functions deployed in Staging without provisioned concurrency enabled (to simulate cold starts); CloudWatch cold-start metric configured
column6:
1. Disable provisioned concurrency on all API Handler functions temporarily
2. Wait 15 min for all warm instances to expire
3. Submit 5 rapid sequential requests to each handler
4. Collect cold-start duration from CloudWatch Lambda metric
5. Re-enable provisioned concurrency
column7: Cold-start duration ≤3 s for all API Handler functions; provisioned concurrency re-enabling eliminates cold starts in subsequent requests
column13: DDD §Business Context – Workload Criticality – Lambda Cold Start ≤3 s; IG §Infrastructure Deployment – Compute
column14: Provisioned concurrency values: isb-api-submit 5; isb-api-status 5; isb-api-retrieve 3; isb-api-admin 2

column3: DynamoDB Read/Write Latency at Target Load
column4: Validate DynamoDB SolutionState and UsageTracking tables meet the ≤10 ms p99 latency target under production workload
column5: DynamoDB tables AmatraISB-SolutionState-Staging and AmatraISB-UsageTracking-Staging active with PAY_PER_REQUEST billing; CloudWatch DynamoDB metrics configured
column6: 1. Execute 2 000 read operations and 500 write operations against both tables via a load script\n2. Collect SuccessfulRequestLatency metric from CloudWatch for a 10-min window\n3. Calculate p99 latency for reads and writes separately
column7: p99 read latency ≤10 ms; p99 write latency ≤10 ms; zero DynamoDB throttle events throughout the run
column13: DDD §Business Context – Workload Criticality – DynamoDB Read/Write ≤10 ms p99; DDD §Infrastructure – Compute Sizing
column14: Matches ~2M reads + 500K writes/month projection from SOW scope parameters

column4: Validate that monthly Bedrock token consumption is tracked and the P3 alarm fires at 80% of the monthly budget
column5: CloudWatch alarm amatra-isb-bedrock-budget-warning-prod configured; SNS subscription for Head of Solutions email active; Bedrock token usage metrics available
column6:
1. Simulate token consumption equal to 80% of the 10M input / 5M output monthly budget in Staging
2. Verify CloudWatch alarm transitions to ALARM state
3. Verify SNS email notification sent to Head of Solutions
4. Reset alarm state and verify it returns to OK
column7: CloudWatch P3 alarm fires at 80% threshold; email notification delivered within 5 min; alarm resets to OK after state reset
column13: DDD §Infrastructure – Monitoring – Alert Definitions P3; DDD §Component Design – AI Generation Engine
column14: Haiku substitution for discovery-questionnaire reduces Sonnet token cost by ~20%

column4: Validate that the API Gateway Cognito authoriser blocks all unauthenticated and tampered token attempts
column5: API Gateway deployed with Cognito authoriser; WAF WebACL with CRS rule group active
column6:
1. Send requests with no Authorization header
2. Send requests with an expired token (>15 min old)
3. Send requests with a token signed by a different Cognito User Pool
4. Send requests with base64-decoded payload tampered then re-encoded
5. Verify all attempts are rejected before Lambda invocation
column7: HTTP 401 for all four unauthorised patterns; no Lambda invocation recorded in CloudWatch; CloudTrail event logged for each rejection
column13: DDD §Security – Identity & Access Management – Authentication; DDD §Integration Design – Authentication & SSO Flows
column14: OWASP A01; verify GuardDuty does not generate false positives

column3: WAF SQL Injection and Rate Limit Validation
column4: Validate that WAF blocks SQL injection payloads and enforces the 2 000 requests per 5-min per-IP rate limit
column5: AWS WAF WebACL with CRS + KBI managed rules attached to API Gateway stage; rate-based rule configured at 2 000 req/5 min/IP
column6:
1. Send POST /api/v1/solutions with SQL injection payload in brief field
2. Verify WAF CRS rule blocks the request
3. Send 2 001 requests from the same IP within a 5-min window using a load script
4. Verify the 2 001st request returns HTTP 429
5. Verify WAF logs record both the SQLi block and the rate-limit block
column7: SQLi payload blocked with HTTP 403 by WAF; rate limit triggers HTTP 429 at request 2 001; all blocks recorded in WAF CloudWatch logs
column13: DDD §Security – Network Security – WAF Rules; IG §Infrastructure Deployment – Networking Validation
column14: OWASP A03; confirm CRS rule group version in use

column4: Validate that all S3 buckets and DynamoDB tables reject unencrypted writes and use the correct CMKs
column5: Four KMS CMKs deployed; S3 bucket policies with deny on unencrypted PutObject; DynamoDB tables encrypted with amatra-isb-dynamodb CMK
column6:
1. Attempt PutObject to artifacts bucket without SSE-KMS header
2. Verify HTTP 403 returned by S3
3. Query each S3 bucket and DynamoDB table for their encryption configuration
4. Verify CMK aliases match alias/amatra-isb-s3-artifacts and alias/amatra-isb-dynamodb respectively
column7: S3 unencrypted PutObject rejected with HTTP 403; all buckets report SSE-KMS with correct CMK alias; both DynamoDB tables report ENABLED encryption with correct CMK
column13: DDD §Security – Data Protection – Encryption at Rest; IG §Infrastructure Deployment – Security Validation Procedures
column14: SOC 2 CC6 evidence item; results documented in evidence package

column4: Validate that API Gateway rejects TLS 1.0 and TLS 1.1 connections and accepts only TLS 1.2 and above
column5: API Gateway custom domain configured with TLS 1.2+ security policy; ACM certificate issued
column6:
1. Attempt HTTPS connection to api.amatra-isb.internal using TLS 1.0
2. Attempt connection using TLS 1.1
3. Attempt connection using TLS 1.2
4. Attempt connection using TLS 1.3
5. Verify response codes and handshake outcomes
column7: TLS 1.0 and TLS 1.1 connections refused with handshake failure; TLS 1.2 connection accepted; TLS 1.3 connection accepted; health endpoint returns HTTP 200 on TLS 1.2+
column13: DDD §Security – Network Security – TLS Enforcement; IG §Infrastructure Deployment – Networking Validation
column14: Run via curl with --tlsv1.0 / --tlsv1.1 / --tlsv1.2 flags

column4: Validate that GuardDuty detects a simulated credential-stuffing event and triggers the automated Lambda remediation response
column5: GuardDuty detector ENABLED; automated remediation Lambda (isb-guardduty-remediation-prod) deployed; SNS topic configured for Security & Compliance Lead notification
column6:
1. Simulate a high-rate Cognito authentication failure pattern exceeding the 10% threshold
2. Verify GuardDuty generates a HIGH severity finding within 5 min
3. Verify automated remediation Lambda suspends the affected Cognito user account
4. Verify SNS notification sent to Security & Compliance Lead
5. Verify event recorded in CloudTrail
column7: GuardDuty HIGH finding raised within 5 min; Cognito user account suspended by remediation Lambda; SNS notification delivered; CloudTrail event logs the suspension
column13: DDD §Security – Monitoring & Threat Detection; DDD §Security – Audit Logging – GuardDuty high-severity remediation
column14: SOC 2 CC7 evidence item

column4: Validate that all Lambda execution roles contain no wildcard resource policies and pass IAM Access Analyzer with zero active findings
column5: IAM Access Analyzer configured for the production account; all Lambda execution roles deployed via iam-lambda-roles.yaml
column6:
1. Run IAM Access Analyzer and retrieve findings for all Lambda execution roles
2. Run automated policy scanner checking for Resource: * statements
3. Verify isb-api-submit-prod role can only access AmatraISB-UsageTracking-Prod and AmatraISB-SolutionState-Prod tables
4. Verify Bedrock invoker role is scoped to named model ARNs only
column7: Zero active IAM Access Analyzer findings; no wildcard resource policies found; isb-api-submit-prod role access limited to specified tables; Bedrock invoker scoped to named ARNs
column13: DDD §Security – Identity & Access Management – Service Accounts; IG §Application Configuration – IAM Role Configuration
column14: Run after every Lambda deployment via CI/CD pipeline gate

column4: Validate that the platform meets the 99.9% monthly availability target measured by the CloudWatch Synthetics canary
column5: CloudWatch Synthetics canary amatra-isb-health-canary deployed with 60-s interval; SNS P1 alarm configured on health-check failure rate
column6:
1. Deploy CloudWatch Synthetics canary pinging GET /api/v1/health every 60 s
2. Monitor canary over a 7-day measurement window in Staging
3. Collect successful request count and failed request count
4. Calculate availability percentage
5. Trigger a simulated Lambda error and verify alarm fires
column7: Availability ≥99.9% over the 7-day window; P1 alarm fires when health-check success rate drops below threshold within 5 min; alarm resolves automatically when service recovers
column13: DDD §Business Context – Workload Criticality – Platform Availability 99.9%; SOW §Success Metrics – Platform Availability
column14: 7-day Staging measurement prior to each production go-live

column4: Validate that DynamoDB Point-in-Time Recovery can restore the SolutionState table to within 15 minutes of a specified timestamp
column5: DynamoDB PITR enabled on AmatraISB-SolutionState-Staging; test data written to the table; IAM permissions for PITR restore granted
column6:
1. Write 10 known solution records to AmatraISB-SolutionState-Staging at T0
2. Record exact timestamp T0
3. Write 5 additional records at T0+5 min
4. Initiate PITR restore to timestamp T0+2 min to a new table AmatraISB-SolutionState-Restored
5. Verify restored table contains exactly 10 original records and not the 5 new ones
column7: Restore completes within 30 min; restored table contains exactly the records written before T0+2 min; no data loss beyond the targeted RPO window of 15 min
column13: DDD §Infrastructure – Disaster Recovery – RPO ≤15 min; DDD §Infrastructure – Disaster Recovery – DR Test; IG §Infrastructure Deployment – Compute – Rollback
column14: Results documented for SOC 2 availability evidence package

column3: Step Functions Retry on Bedrock Throttling
column4: Validate that the Step Functions state machine retries the Bedrock invocation task up to 3 times with exponential backoff on ThrottlingException
column5: Step Functions state machine deployed with retry config: maxAttempts=3; interval=30s; backOffRate=2; Bedrock invocation task configured; a mock Lambda that returns ThrottlingException is available
column6:
1. Substitute a mock Bedrock invoker Lambda that returns ThrottlingException for the first 2 invocations then succeeds on the 3rd
2. Start a Step Functions execution
3. Monitor execution timeline for retry intervals
4. Verify execution completes successfully on the 3rd attempt
column7: Execution retries 2 times before succeeding; retry intervals approximately 30 s and 60 s (exponential backoff); final execution status SUCCEEDED; no DLQ message generated
column13: DDD §Integration Design – Messaging & Event Patterns – Retry Policy; DDD §Integration Design – External System Integrations
column14: Validates async job durability for the 30–60 min job window

column4: Validate that a failed job message triggers the P1 DLQ alarm and can be successfully replayed to the primary queue
column5: SQS DLQ amatra-isb-job-dlq-staging active with maxReceiveCount=3; CloudWatch P1 alarm on DLQ depth > 0; SNS PagerDuty integration configured in Staging using a test endpoint
column6:
1. Place a synthetic message that will fail processing onto amatra-isb-job-queue-staging
2. Allow the message to exhaust 3 receive attempts and move to DLQ
3. Verify CloudWatch P1 alarm transitions to ALARM
4. Run replay_dlq_message.py script to move message back to primary queue
5. Verify message is processed successfully on replay
column7: P1 alarm fires within 5 min of message arriving in DLQ; SNS notification delivered; replayed message processed successfully with job reaching COMPLETE; DLQ depth returns to 0
column13: DDD §Integration Design – Messaging & Event Patterns – Dead Letter Queue; IG §Appendix D – Troubleshooting – DLQ Message Received
column14: Runbook RUN-001 validation

column4: Validate that CloudTrail captures all required API calls and that log file integrity validation detects no tampering
column5: CloudTrail trail amatra-isb-cloudtrail-prod active for management events and S3 data events; S3 Object Lock enabled on log bucket with 7-year Compliance mode retention
column6:
1. Perform 10 auditable platform actions (brief submission; usage override; Cognito user change; S3 PutObject)
2. Wait 15 min for CloudTrail log delivery
3. Run aws cloudtrail validate-logs for the log period
4. Verify each of the 10 actions appears in CloudTrail events
5. Attempt to delete a log file and verify Object Lock prevents deletion
column7: All 10 auditable actions present in CloudTrail; validate-logs returns zero failures; delete attempt rejected by Object Lock with HTTP 403
column13: DDD §Security – Audit Logging & SIEM Integration; DDD §Security – Compliance Mappings SOC 2 CC7
column14: SOC 2 evidence item; run monthly per ongoing monitoring schedule

column4: Validate that the admin right-to-erasure Lambda deletes all user-linked S3 objects; DynamoDB SolutionState records; DynamoDB UsageTracking records; and the Cognito UserRecord for a given user_id
column5: Test user with 3 solutions in SolutionState; 2 months of UsageTracking records; Cognito account; and S3 artifacts written; admin erasure Lambda deployed
column6:
1. Record all S3 keys; DynamoDB records; and Cognito sub for the test user
2. Invoke the admin erasure Lambda with the test user_id
3. Verify all S3 objects under the user's solution keys are deleted (versioned)
4. Verify DynamoDB SolutionState and UsageTracking records removed
5. Verify Cognito UserRecord deleted
column7: All S3 objects deleted (S3 version markers created for audit); DynamoDB records removed; Cognito account deleted; CloudTrail logs the erasure events; no personal data remains in any store
column13: DDD §Data Architecture – Data Governance – Right to Erasure GDPR; DDD §Security – Compliance Mappings GDPR Article 25
column14: GDPR Article 17 compliance test; execute annually in Staging

column4: Validate that a pre-sales consultant can submit a real (PII-scrubbed) client brief and receive a complete pre-sales engagement package meeting Amatra's quality standards
column5: UAT environment with platform deployed; Cognito UAT user in PreSales group; 3-5 representative PII-scrubbed client briefs provided by Head of Solutions; quality rubric defined
column6:
1. Pre-sales consultant logs in via Cognito
2. Submits one of the provided client briefs via the platform
3. Polls job status until COMPLETE
4. Downloads all 4 Phase 1 artifact types
5. Scores each artifact against the quality rubric
